bylaws.title: Off Road Vehicle & All-terrain Vehicles April 20, 2004
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law allows off-road vehicles and ATVs with three or more wheels to legally drive on Township roads. It makes these vehicles street-legal within Nipissing Township's road system.
ai_key_points: Off-road vehicles and ATVs with 3+ wheels can drive on Township roads
Vehicles must have low-pressure balloon-type tires (maximum 70 kpa/10 psi)
ATVs must have handlebars, straddled seat, and carry driver only
Must follow provincial Highway Traffic Act regulations
Highway Traffic Act penalties apply for violations

bylaws.title: Sale of Land Policy
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes the rules and procedures the Township must follow when selling municipal land. It ensures transparency through public notice requirements and fair market pricing through appraisals.
ai_key_points: Council must declare land 'surplus' before it can be sold
Public notice must be given before any land sale
Professional appraisals are required to determine fair market value
Council can choose sale method: public tender, real estate agent, or direct sale
Some small parcels and transfers to other governments don't need appraisals

bylaws.title: Trailer Use (Temporary during Construction)
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law creates rules for using trailers as temporary homes while building a permanent residence. It establishes a policy with specific requirements and application procedures for residents who need temporary housing during construction.
ai_key_points: Allows trailers as temporary residences only during home construction
Requires following Township policy with specific requirements
Must use official application process with sample letter provided
Policy details are in attached Schedule A document

bylaws.title: Entrance 911 By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law requires all properties with entrances or buildings to display official civic address numbers on standardized signs to help emergency services locate addresses. The Township installs the initial sign but property owners are responsible for maintenance costs.
ai_key_points: Every property with an entrance or building must display a Township-assigned civic address number
Signs must be clearly visible from the road using specific materials and design
Township installs the initial sign and adds cost to building/entrance permits
Property owners must maintain signs in good repair or pay replacement costs
Unpaid sign costs are added to property tax bills after 30 days

bylaws.title: Authorize Speed Limit Changes
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law sets a default speed limit of 60 km/h on all municipal roads in Nipissing Township that don't already have posted speed limit signs. It helps ensure consistent speed limits across the township.
ai_key_points: Speed limit is 60 km/h on all unposted township roads
Only applies to roads under township jurisdiction, not provincial highways
Standard Highway Traffic Act penalties apply for speeding violations
Council held public meeting for input before passing the by-law
Passed in December 2013 following public consultation

bylaws.title: Amend BL 1088 – Off Road Vehicles on Municipal Roads
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law allows off-road vehicles (like ATVs) to be driven on Township of Nipissing municipal roads, replacing the previous rules. It sets speed limits and equipment requirements while prohibiting use in parks, cemeteries, and community centers.
ai_key_points: Off-road vehicles are now permitted on municipal roads within the township
Speed limit is 20 km/h on roads with 50 km/h limit, 50 km/h on faster roads
Vehicles must meet provincial equipment requirements under Ontario regulations
Prohibited in parks, beaches, cemeteries, and community centers except parking areas
Violating this by-law is an offence with legal penalties

bylaws.title: Minimum Maintenance Standards – Roads
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes minimum standards for maintaining Township roads based on traffic levels and speed limits. It classifies roads into different categories and sets requirements for snow plowing, repairs, and emergency weather responses.
ai_key_points: Roads are classified into different maintenance categories based on daily traffic counts and speed limits
Township must maintain roads to minimum provincial standards but makes no promise to exceed them
During severe weather events, the Township will declare alerts and adjust road maintenance services
Road classifications and maintenance standards are reviewed every five years based on traffic counts
Private roads require upgrade to Township standards before being accepted into the municipal system

bylaws.title: Amending Maximum Weight on Bridges
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law updates weight limits for vehicles crossing the Hummel Bridge on Hemlock Road between Nipissing Township and Powassan. It sets different maximum weights based on vehicle type to protect the bridge structure.
ai_key_points: Applies to Hummel Bridge (Bridge No. 6) on Hemlock Road at the Powassan boundary
L1 vehicles cannot exceed 33 tonnes gross weight
L2 vehicles cannot exceed 23 tonnes gross weight
L3 vehicles cannot exceed 17 tonnes gross weight
Updates previous weight limits from by-law 2019-42

bylaws.title: Boundary Road Agreement – Powassan
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes an agreement with the Municipality of Powassan regarding the maintenance and responsibilities for boundary roads that are shared between the two municipalities.
ai_key_points: Formalizes shared responsibility for roads along the municipal boundary with Powassan
Establishes maintenance duties and cost-sharing arrangements between municipalities
Defines which municipality handles specific sections of boundary roads
Sets framework for ongoing cooperation on shared infrastructure

bylaws.title: Public Dock By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law regulates how residents can use the Township's three public boat docks (Wades Landing, Chapman's Landing, and McQuaby Lake Launch). It sets time limits and rules to ensure everyone has fair access to these public facilities.
ai_key_points: Boats can only be parked at docks for up to 4 hours continuously
No overnight parking allowed between 11:00 p.m. and 7:00 a.m.
Materials can only be stored on docks for 1 hour maximum
Commercial boats and barges need special permission from Township office
Violations result in $150 fines ($400 for unauthorized commercial use)

bylaws.title: Landfill By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes rules and regulations for the Township of Nipissing's landfill operations. It sets out what materials can be disposed of, operating procedures, and requirements for residents and businesses using the landfill facility.
ai_key_points: Specifies what waste materials are accepted and prohibited at the landfill
Sets operating hours and procedures for landfill use
Establishes fees and charges for different types of waste disposal
Outlines safety requirements and responsibilities for landfill users
Provides penalties for violations of landfill regulations

bylaws.title: By-Law Amending Minimum Maintenance Standards Schedule E
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law changes how the Township assigns snow plows to specific routes during winter. It removes the requirement to assign specific truck numbers to particular roads, giving the Township more flexibility to respond to winter weather conditions.
ai_key_points: Amends the Township's winter road maintenance standards from 2019
Removes requirement to assign specific plow trucks to designated routes
Allows more flexible snow plow deployment during winter storms
Helps Township respond more efficiently to winter weather events
Takes effect immediately upon passing in December 2020

bylaws.title: Recreation Committee
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law creates a Recreation Committee to manage the Township's recreational programs and Community Centre at 2381 Highway 654. The committee oversees facility operations, sets user fees, and administers fitness and recreation services for residents.
ai_key_points: Committee includes 2 council members and 4-8 township residents, appointed annually
Manages Community Centre operations including fitness centre and recreational programming
Sets facility usage fees subject to council approval
Must submit annual budget to council by February 1st
Holds minimum 4 meetings per year with elected chairperson and secretary

bylaws.title: Cemetery Committee
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law creates a Cemetery Committee to manage the township's three cemeteries. The committee will handle day-to-day operations, set rules, and manage budgets for these burial grounds.
ai_key_points: Committee manages Nipissing Union, Commanda, and St. John's Alsace cemeteries
Five members: two councillors and three township residents
Committee sets cemetery rules and submits annual budget to Council
Must hold at least one meeting per year
Committee members appointed after each municipal election

bylaws.title: Community Safety Zone
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law creates a Community Safety Zone around South Shore Education Centre with reduced speed limits and doubled fines for speeding violations to protect students and residents.
ai_key_points: Speed limit is 40 km/h on Beatty Street, Front Street, Blake Street, and Powerplant Road
Zone covers area around elementary school, park, and outdoor ice rink
Speeding fines are doubled in this designated safety zone
OPP will enforce the speed limits and safety rules
Zone operates 24/7 year-round for enhanced safety

bylaws.title: Parking By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law sets parking rules for all public roads and municipal property in Nipissing Township. It establishes where you can and cannot park, creates emergency zones, and sets penalties for violations.
ai_key_points: No parking within 3 metres of driveways or 9 metres of intersections
48-hour maximum parking limit on any roadway
Emergency Loading Zones at beaches and boat launches — vehicles will be towed
Winter parking restrictions at turnaround areas from October 31 to May 1
Fines range from $30-75, with vehicles subject to towing at owner's expense

bylaws.title: Unopened Road Allowances By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes rules for managing unopened road allowances in Nipissing Township. It likely addresses how these undeveloped road spaces can be used, maintained, or potentially closed by the municipality.
ai_key_points: Regulates the use and management of unopened road allowances throughout the township
Establishes procedures for potentially closing or disposing of unused road allowances
May restrict public access or activities on these undeveloped road spaces
Provides framework for municipal decision-making regarding future road development

bylaws.title: Amend Zoning By-Law 2020-20 Section 3.44 Trailers
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law updates the rules about where trailers can be parked or stored on properties in Nipissing Township. It changes the existing zoning regulations to clarify trailer placement requirements.
ai_key_points: Modifies existing rules for trailer parking and storage on residential properties
Updates Section 3.44 of the main zoning by-law from 2020
Changes may affect where residents can keep RVs, travel trailers, or utility trailers
New regulations help clarify what was previously allowed under zoning rules

bylaws.title: Culvert/Entrance
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law regulates how property owners can install driveways that connect to municipal roads, including required culverts for drainage. You must get Township approval before installing any entrance to your property from a municipal road.
ai_key_points: Must get approval from Township Operations Superintendent before installing any driveway entrance
Required culverts must be new, minimum 400mm diameter, 9 metres long
Property owner pays for and installs culvert according to Township specifications
Only one entrance allowed per residential dwelling
Work must start within 6 months of permit approval

bylaws.title: Procedural By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes the rules and procedures for how Township of Nipissing council meetings are conducted, including meeting schedules, voting procedures, and council member conduct.
ai_key_points: Sets rules for council meetings including quorum requirements and voting procedures
Establishes when meetings are held and how they're called
Defines roles of Mayor, Deputy Mayor, and council members during meetings
Requires council members to follow conflict of interest rules
Creates procedures for public participation and presentations to council

bylaws.title: Cemetery Regulations
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes rules and regulations for cemetery operations and use within the Township of Nipissing. It sets standards for burials, grave maintenance, and cemetery administration to ensure respectful and orderly management of municipal cemeteries.
ai_key_points: Establishes rules for burial procedures and grave site preparation
Sets requirements for headstones, monuments and grave decorations
Defines cemetery operating hours and visitor conduct rules
Outlines maintenance responsibilities for grave sites and cemetery grounds
Specifies fees and permits required for cemetery services

bylaws.title: Dog By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law sets rules for dog ownership in Nipissing Township, including licensing requirements, leash laws, and owner responsibilities. It helps ensure public safety and responsible pet ownership in the community.
ai_key_points: All dogs must be licensed with the Township
Dogs must be leashed in public areas and under owner control
Owners must clean up after their dogs in public spaces
Penalties and fines apply for violations of dog ownership rules

bylaws.title: Boundary Road Agreement – Callander
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes an agreement with the Municipality of Callander regarding a shared boundary road. It sets out responsibilities for maintenance, costs, and management of the road that serves both communities.
ai_key_points: Creates formal agreement between Nipissing Township and Callander for boundary road management
Establishes shared responsibilities for road maintenance and repair costs
Defines which municipality handles specific aspects of road upkeep
Ensures coordinated approach to road services affecting both communities

bylaws.title: Accountability & Transparency
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes rules for how the Township of Nipissing must operate transparently and be accountable to residents. It ensures public access to information and responsible government decision-making.
ai_key_points: Requires public meetings and proper notice for council decisions
Establishes procedures for residents to access municipal records and information
Sets standards for financial reporting and budget transparency
Creates accountability measures for municipal staff and elected officials

bylaws.title: Delegation of Authority
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law allows Township Council to delegate certain decision-making powers and responsibilities to municipal staff, committees, or other officials. This helps streamline operations and allows routine matters to be handled more efficiently without requiring full Council approval.
ai_key_points: Council can delegate specific powers to staff and committees for routine decisions
Helps speed up municipal processes without requiring full Council meetings
Staff can make certain administrative decisions on behalf of Council
Council retains oversight and can revoke delegated authorities if needed

bylaws.title: Regulate Sale of Land
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes rules and procedures for how the Township of Nipissing can sell municipal land. It protects public interests by ensuring land sales follow proper processes and requirements.
ai_key_points: Sets procedures Township must follow when selling municipal property
Ensures transparency and fairness in land sale processes
Protects public interest in disposal of municipal assets
Establishes requirements for notices and public consultation
Creates framework for evaluating and approving land sales

bylaws.title: Nuisance Beaver By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law allows the Township to manage beaver dams that pose flood risks to roads and municipal property. Property owners must remove beaver dams that create flooding hazards or face enforcement action and costs.
ai_key_points: Property owners cannot allow beaver dams that create flood risks or threaten public safety
Township can order immediate removal of beaver dams threatening municipal roads or infrastructure
If owners don't comply, Township can remove dams and charge costs to property taxes
Township officials can inspect properties and use drones to check for beaver dam issues
Violations can result in fines under the Provincial Offences Act

bylaws.title: Recreation Committee
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes a Recreation Committee for the Township of Nipissing to oversee and make recommendations about recreational programs, facilities, and services in the community.
ai_key_points: Creates an official Recreation Committee to advise Township Council on recreational matters
Committee will help plan and oversee local recreation programs and facilities
Provides formal structure for community input on recreational services and activities

bylaws.title: Museum Board
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes or updates the rules for the Township's Museum Board, including how it operates, who can serve on it, and what responsibilities it has for managing the local museum.
ai_key_points: Establishes the structure and governance of the Township's Museum Board
Sets out qualifications and appointment process for board members
Defines the board's responsibilities for museum operations and programming
Outlines meeting procedures and decision-making processes for the board

bylaws.title: Cemetery Committee
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes a Cemetery Committee to oversee and manage cemetery operations in the Township of Nipissing. The committee will make decisions about cemetery policies, maintenance, and administration to ensure proper care of burial grounds.
ai_key_points: Creates a Cemetery Committee to manage township cemetery operations and policies
Committee oversees maintenance and care of municipal burial grounds
Establishes governance structure for cemetery-related decisions and administration
Ensures proper management of cemetery services for residents

bylaws.title: Establishing and Regulating Fire Department
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law officially establishes and continues the Township of Nipissing Fire Department, defining its structure, services, and operations. It outlines how the fire department will provide fire protection, emergency response, and fire prevention services to residents.
ai_key_points: Continues the Township of Nipissing Fire Department with a Fire Chief as head
Fire department provides fire suppression, rescue, emergency response, and fire prevention services
Property owners may be charged costs for special fire/emergency response services and equipment
Fire Chief enforces fire safety laws and conducts fire prevention and public education
Department includes volunteer firefighters with specific training and promotion requirements

bylaws.title: Social Media Policy
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes rules for how Township employees and officials use social media when representing the municipality. It protects the Township's reputation and ensures professional online conduct.
ai_key_points: Applies to all Township employees and elected officials using social media
Sets guidelines for professional conduct when posting about Township business
Protects confidential information from being shared online
Establishes consequences for inappropriate social media use
Helps maintain public trust in municipal government

bylaws.title: Code of Conduct
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes rules for how Township council members and local board members must behave in their official roles. It ensures elected officials act ethically and professionally when serving residents.
ai_key_points: Sets ethical standards and professional conduct rules for council members and board appointees
Requires officials to act in the public interest and avoid conflicts of interest
Establishes complaint procedures for residents who believe officials violated the code
May include penalties like reprimands or suspension of pay for code violations

bylaws.title: Trailer Licensing By-Law
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law requires residents to get a license to keep trailers on their property for more than 21 days per year. It regulates where trailers can be placed and ensures proper sewage management.
ai_key_points: Must get license if trailer stays on property more than 21 consecutive days yearly
Trailers only for recreational use — no permanent living allowed December 1 to April 30
Only one trailer per property, must follow setback rules from zoning by-law
Need proper sewage system connection or approved outhouse and greywater pit
Two license types: annual (full year) or short-term (1-3 months between May-November)

bylaws.title: provide for an Interim Tax Levy
bylaws.ai_summary: The Township of Nipissing set temporary property tax rates for 2024 until the final budget is approved. Property owners must pay these interim taxes by the due dates.
ai_key_points: Property owners must pay interim property taxes for 2024
Tax rates are temporary until final budget is approved
Payment due dates are set by the municipality
Applies to all taxable properties in Township of Nipissing

bylaws.title: appoint an Emergency Management Program Committee for the Township of Nipissing
bylaws.votes: Moved by D. Yemm, Seconded by T. Butler
bylaws.ai_summary: The Township is creating a special committee to handle emergency planning and response. This committee will help prepare for and manage emergencies in the community.
ai_key_points: Creates an Emergency Management Program Committee for the Township
Committee will handle emergency planning and disaster response coordination
Establishes formal structure for community emergency preparedness

bylaws.title: confirm the proceedings of Council at its meeting held on January 2, 2024
bylaws.votes: Moved by S. Kirkey, Seconded by D. Yemm
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law officially confirms and makes legally valid all decisions, motions, and actions that Township Council made during their January 2, 2024 meeting.
ai_key_points: Confirms all Council decisions made at the January 2, 2024 meeting are legally valid
Makes all motions and resolutions passed that day officially part of township law
Ensures proper legal procedures were followed for all Council actions

bylaws.title: confirm the proceedings of Council at its special meeting held on January 10, 2024
bylaws.votes: Moved by S. Kirkey, Seconded by T. Butler
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law officially confirms and makes legal all decisions and actions that Township Council made during a special meeting on January 10, 2024.
ai_key_points: Confirms all Council decisions from January 10, 2024 special meeting are valid
Makes official any resolutions or motions passed during that meeting
Standard procedural by-law with no penalties or new regulations for residents

bylaws.title: amend Zoning By-Law 2020-20, as amended
bylaws.votes: Moved by S. Kirkey, Seconded by S. Foote
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law makes changes to the Township of Nipissing's zoning rules from 2020. It updates what can be built where and under what conditions.
ai_key_points: Updates existing zoning rules that control what types of buildings and activities are allowed in different areas
Changes requirements for property development, building setbacks, heights, or permitted uses
Modifies zoning classifications or boundaries within the township
Alters development standards that property owners must follow when building or renovating

bylaws.title: confirm the proceedings of Council at its meeting held on January 16, 2024
bylaws.votes: Moved by T. Butler, Seconded by S. Kirkey
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law officially confirms and makes legal all decisions and actions that Township Council made during their meeting on January 16, 2024.
ai_key_points: Makes all Council decisions from January 16, 2024 meeting officially valid and legal
Standard administrative procedure to formalize Council proceedings
No specific regulations, requirements, or penalties for residents

bylaws.title: Providing Public Notice under theMunicipal Act, 2001
bylaws.votes: Moved by S. Kirkey, Seconded by S. Foote
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law establishes how the Township of Nipissing will notify residents about municipal matters, meetings, and decisions as required by provincial law. It ensures residents receive proper notice about issues that affect them.
ai_key_points: Sets rules for how the township must inform residents about municipal business
Ensures compliance with Provincial Municipal Act notification requirements
Establishes standard procedures for public notices and announcements
Helps residents stay informed about local government decisions and meetings

bylaws.title: appoint a Building Inspector for the purpose of the enforcement of the Building Code Act, its regulations and the Building By-Law for the Corporati...
bylaws.votes: Moved by S. Foote, Seconded by D. Yemm
bylaws.ai_summary: The Township of Nipissing appointed a Building Inspector to enforce building codes and regulations. This person ensures construction projects follow proper safety standards and building rules.
ai_key_points: A Building Inspector is officially appointed to oversee construction in the township
The inspector enforces Building Code Act rules and local building bylaws
All building projects must comply with provincial and municipal building standards
The inspector has authority to ensure construction meets safety requirements

bylaws.title: confirm the proceedings of Council at its meeting held on February 6, 2024
bylaws.votes: Moved by T. Butler, Seconded by S. Foote
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law officially confirms and makes legally valid all decisions and actions that Township Council made during their February 6, 2024 meeting.
ai_key_points: Makes all Council decisions from February 6, 2024 meeting officially legal
Standard administrative procedure to formalize Council proceedings
No new regulations or requirements for residents

bylaws.title: amend By-Law Number 2022-34, cited as the “Procedural By-Law” to update the public notice guidelines for meetings
bylaws.votes: Moved by T. Butler, Seconded by S. Kirkey
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law updates the rules for how the Township must notify residents about upcoming public meetings. It changes the existing meeting procedures to improve public notice requirements.
ai_key_points: Updates how the Township notifies residents about public meetings
Changes existing meeting procedure rules from 2022
Modifies public notice guidelines and requirements
Ensures residents get proper advance notice of meetings

bylaws.title: Amend By-Law number 2021-50, to regulate the parking of vehicles within the Corporation of the Township of Nipissing
bylaws.votes: Moved by D. Yemm, Seconded by S. Foote
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law updates the Township of Nipissing's parking rules from 2021. It changes where and how vehicles can be parked on municipal property and roads.
ai_key_points: Updates existing parking regulations from By-Law 2021-50
Controls where vehicles can park on township roads and property
May include new parking restrictions or permit requirements
Violations likely result in fines or towing
Applies to all vehicles within Township of Nipissing boundaries

bylaws.title: Amend By-Law Number 2019-41, cited as the Township of Nipissing Road Status and Maintenance By-Law
bylaws.votes: Moved by S. Kirkey, Seconded by T. Butler
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law updates the Township's road maintenance rules from 2019. It changes how roads are classified and maintained throughout Nipissing Township.
ai_key_points: Updates the 2019 road maintenance by-law with new rules
Changes how township roads are classified and categorized
Modifies maintenance standards and requirements for different road types
Affects road service levels provided to residents

bylaws.title: confirm the proceedings of Council at its meeting held on February 20, 2024
bylaws.votes: Moved by S. Foote, Seconded by D. Yemm
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law officially confirms and makes legal all decisions and actions taken by Township Council during their February 20, 2024 meeting.
ai_key_points: Confirms all resolutions and decisions made at Council's February 20, 2024 meeting
Makes all Council proceedings from that meeting officially valid and legal
Standard administrative procedure to formalize Council business

bylaws.title: appoint an Interim Deputy Clerk for the Township of Nipissing
bylaws.votes: Moved by D. Yemm, Seconded by S. Foote
bylaws.ai_summary: The Township of Nipissing has appointed someone to temporarily fill the Deputy Clerk position. This person will handle deputy clerk duties until a permanent replacement is found.
ai_key_points: Appoints a temporary Deputy Clerk for the Township
Person will perform all deputy clerk duties during interim period
Position is temporary until permanent deputy clerk is hired

bylaws.title: confirm the proceedings of Council at its meeting held on March 5, 2024
bylaws.votes: Moved by S. Kirkey, Seconded by S. Foote
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law officially confirms and validates all decisions, motions, and actions that Township Council made during their March 5, 2024 meeting.
ai_key_points: Makes all Council decisions from March 5, 2024 meeting legally binding
Confirms all motions and votes passed by Council that day
Standard administrative procedure to validate Council proceedings

bylaws.title: confirm the proceedings of Council at its meeting held on March 19, 2024
bylaws.votes: Moved by S. Kirkey, Seconded by T. Butler
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law officially confirms and makes legally binding all decisions, motions, and actions taken by Township Council during their March 19, 2024 meeting.
ai_key_points: Confirms all council decisions made at March 19, 2024 meeting are official
Makes council proceedings from that meeting legally binding
Standard administrative by-law to formalize council actions

bylaws.title: adopt a Human Resources Policy and Procedure Manual
bylaws.votes: Moved by T. Butler, Seconded by S. Foote
bylaws.ai_summary: The Township of Nipissing has adopted a new Human Resources Policy and Procedure Manual that sets rules for how township employees are managed and treated at work.
ai_key_points: Creates official HR policies for township employees covering workplace rules and procedures
Establishes guidelines for hiring, managing, and disciplining municipal staff
Sets standards for employee conduct and workplace expectations
Replaces any previous HR policies with this new comprehensive manual

bylaws.title: confirm the proceedings of Council at its meeting held on April 2, 2024
bylaws.votes: Moved by T. Butler, Seconded by D. Yemm
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law officially confirms that all decisions and actions taken by Township Council at their April 2, 2024 meeting are valid and legally binding.
ai_key_points: Makes all Council decisions from April 2, 2024 meeting officially valid
Standard procedure to ensure Council actions have legal force
No new regulations or requirements for residents

bylaws.title: confirm the proceedings of Council at its meeting held on April 16, 2024
bylaws.votes: Moved by S. Kirkey, Seconded by S. Foote
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law officially confirms and validates all decisions, resolutions, and actions taken by Township Council during their April 16, 2024 meeting.
ai_key_points: Makes all Council decisions from April 16, 2024 meeting legally binding
Confirms all resolutions and votes taken during that Council session
Standard administrative procedure to validate Council proceedings

bylaws.title: appoint a Councillor
bylaws.votes: Moved by D. Yemm, Seconded by S. Foote
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law appoints a new councillor to fill a vacant position on Township of Nipissing council.
ai_key_points: Appoints a specific person to serve as councillor
Fills a vacant council seat
Person will have full councillor duties and voting rights

bylaws.title: confirm the proceedings of Council at its meeting held on May 9, 2024
bylaws.votes: Moved by D. Yemm, Seconded by S. Kirkey
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law officially confirms and makes legally valid all decisions and actions taken by Township Council during their May 9, 2024 meeting.
ai_key_points: Makes all Council decisions from May 9, 2024 meeting official and legally binding
Confirms any motions, resolutions, or votes passed during that Council session
Standard procedural by-law to formalize Council proceedings into municipal law

bylaws.title: adopt the estimates for all sums required for the year, to provide for the adoption of tax rates and to further provide for penalty and interest in...
bylaws.votes: Moved by S. Kirkey, Seconded by D. Yemm
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law sets the Township's 2024 budget, establishes property tax rates for different types of properties, and sets penalties and interest charges for late tax payments.
ai_key_points: Sets 2024 budget amounts for all township spending and services
Establishes property tax rates for residential, commercial, and other property types
Creates penalty charges for late property tax payments
Sets interest rates on overdue tax amounts
Provides legal framework for collecting property taxes

bylaws.title: set tax ratios for Municipal Purposes for the year 2024
bylaws.votes: Moved by D. Yemm, Seconded by J. Scott
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law sets the tax rates that different types of properties will pay for municipal services in 2024. It determines how much residential, commercial, and industrial properties are taxed relative to each other.
ai_key_points: Sets official tax ratios for different property types in Nipissing Township for 2024
Determines relative tax rates between residential, commercial, and industrial properties
Establishes how municipal costs are divided among different property classes
Required annual process to set fair taxation levels for municipal services

bylaws.title: amend the Remuneration Schedule in the Township of Nipissing Pay Equity Plan
bylaws.votes: Moved by S. Kirkey, Seconded by S. Foote
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law updates the pay rates in the Township's Pay Equity Plan to ensure fair compensation between male and female employees in similar jobs.
ai_key_points: Updates salary schedules to comply with pay equity requirements
Ensures equal pay for jobs of equal value between genders
Amends existing compensation structure for Township employees

bylaws.title: adopt the Municipal Position Descriptions for municipal staff positions for the Township of Nipissing
bylaws.votes: Moved by S. Foote, Seconded by D. Yemm
bylaws.ai_summary: This by-law officially creates and adopts job descriptions for all municipal staff positions in the Township of Nipissing for 2024.
ai_key_points: Establishes official job descriptions for all township staff positions
Defines roles, responsibilities, and requirements for municipal employees
Provides framework for hiring, performance evaluation, and job expectations
Ensures standardized employment practices across all township departments

bylaws.title: confirm the appointment of members to the Township of Nipissing Committee of Adjustment
bylaws.votes: Moved by J. Scott, Seconded by S. Kirkey
